Cat® Energy Control System (ECS) 200 – Documentation Suite

The Cat® ECS 200 is a paralleling generator set controller designed for multi-unit power systems in island and utility paralleling applications. Built on a single, versatile hardware platform, it supports a broad range of genset control scenarios — from standalone emergency power to complex multi-generator paralleling switchgear with automatic load management. The system is configured and monitored through a dedicated touchscreen HMI, the Cat Electronic Technician (ET) service tool, and remote SCADA (Modbus TCP/IP) connections.

This documentation suite comprises 5 specification manuals totaling 979 pages, covering the controller hardware, HMI operator interface, core paralleling genset software, Balance of Plant (BOP) ancillary controls for CHP/container applications, and the SCADA Modbus data interface. Together they define the complete system from physical installation through software configuration to remote supervisory integration.

Document Library — Detailed Summaries

LEBE20713-01 · 29 pages · ©2024

🔧 Controller Hardware Manual

Defines the physical hardware platform shared by both the ECS 100 and ECS 200 product lines. The controller is a single-board module whose functionality is determined entirely by the loaded application software. This manual focuses exclusively on the electrical and mechanical specifications — software behaviour is covered in separate documents.

Physical Specifications
Dimensions286 × 200 × 51 mm (81 mm with tallest connector)
Operating Temp–40 °C to +70 °C
Max Surrounding50 °C
Ingress ProtectionIP20 (packaged controller)
Communication Interfaces
  • 4 × 4-wire Ethernet ports (10/100 Mbps)
  • 2 × 2-wire Ethernet ports (SPE / 10BASE-T1L)
  • 1 × Primary CAN bus port
  • 1 × Accessory CAN bus port
  • 1 × RS-485 serial port
Signal Inputs
  • 12 × Discrete digital inputs
  • 1 × Isolated digital input
  • 3 × Analog inputs (resistive or voltage mode, shared 5V supply)
  • 1 × MPU (Magnetic Pick-Up) speed sensing input
  • 2 × 3-phase AC voltage sensing inputs (generator + bus/utility)
  • 1 × 3-phase AC current sensing input (via CTs)
Signal Outputs
  • 12 × Sinking digital outputs
  • 2 × Analog outputs (voltage or current mode)
  • 3 × PWM outputs

The manual covers mounting/installation procedures with DIN rail and panel-mount options, grounding requirements, wire gauge recommendations (14–20 AWG depending on signal type, shielded twisted pair for communications), power supply requirements, LED indicator meanings (power/firmware status, CAN 1/2 communication), AC metering accuracy specifications, and the programming/firmware update interface.

🖥️ HMI Manual – Paralleling Genset Control

Complete operator interface documentation for the ECS 200 touchscreen HMI used in paralleling generator set applications. The HMI connects to the controller over Ethernet and provides real-time system visualization, parameter configuration, event management, and security-controlled access to all operational and service-level functions.

Physical & Installation
  • Panel-mount touchscreen with specified panel cutout dimensions
  • Power requirements and mechanical specifications
  • Operating temperature range for display and backlight
  • Ethernet communication wiring guidelines
  • HMI software flashing / update procedure
  • Cleaning guidelines for touchscreen surface
Navigation & User Interface
  • Menu Button – Main menu with two levels of submenus
  • Home Button – Return to system overview
  • Events Button – Active alarms & event history
  • Alarm Acknowledgement – Acknowledge active alarms
  • Lock/Unlock – Screen lock with timeout
  • ECS Button – Engine Control Switch (Off/Manual/Auto)
  • Wayfinding text, annunciator strip, context buttons
System Parameter Screens
  • System Overview – Multi-unit plant view with utility, bus bars, breaker status, real power
  • Paralleling – Dead Bus Arbitration, Sync Overview, Load Share, Load Sense Load Demand (LSLD)
  • Genset / Engine / AC – Per-unit status, oil pressure, coolant temp, voltage, current, frequency, power
  • I/O Overview – Digital inputs/outputs, analog I/O, PWM, remote DI/DO/AI/AO/RTD, custom maps
  • Voltage Regulator – IVR status and settings
  • Network Status – Module communication health
  • kW Load Histogram – Historical load distribution
Control & Configuration
  • Control Features – Paralleling, idle/rated, load shed, fuel transfer, AMF, cycle timer, gas blending
  • Annunciator – LED bar, alarm outputs, icon bar, custom events, lamp test
  • Custom Screens – User-designed HMI screens
  • Configurable Parameters – DBA, load share, generator ratings, voltage reg, Ethernet, SCADA, engine management
  • Security – Multi-level password management (user/service levels), enter/change/edit password workflows
  • Remote HMI – Remote display capability, flash file, remote password management

Additional topics include loss of communication behaviour (how the HMI recovers when controller connection drops), remote sensing for utility monitoring, hardware block diagrams showing system interconnections, and HMI file manager for managing configuration files and on-screen PDF manuals. The manual also covers display pairing for genset remote display scenarios and Ethernet remote display setup using an ET-connected laptop.

⚙️ Software Operations Manual – Paralleling Generator Set Control

The most comprehensive document in the suite (420 pages, 42 major sections), this manual defines the complete software behaviour of the ECS 200 when configured as a paralleling generator set controller. It covers everything from initial electrical connections and basic parameter setup through advanced island/utility paralleling algorithms, integrated voltage regulation, PLC logic programming, and functional safety certification requirements.

Generator Set Configuration
  • Engine Sensors – Oil pressure, coolant temperature sensor mapping and threshold configuration
  • Generator Ratings – Ratings groups, output voltage, frequency, and power settings
  • Engine Speed – Speed control, idle/rated speed settings, MPU configuration
  • Generator Protection – Overcurrent, voltage/current imbalance, over/under frequency, over/under voltage, reverse real power, reverse reactive power, over/under reactive power, open phase detection, pole slip detection, utility RoCoF
  • AC Sensing – Voltage and current measurement configuration and calibration
  • Setpoint Save/Restore – Configuration backup and restore procedures
Start/Stop & Engine Control
  • Automatic Control – Auto mode with remote start/stop via DI or SCADA
  • Cranking Control – Controller-controlled cranking sequences with attempt/rest timers
  • Fuel Priming – Pre-start fuel priming setpoints and timers
  • Starter Motor Protection – Over-crank protection and starter engage/disengage logic
  • Emergency Stop – E-stop input handling and shutdown sequences
  • Cooldown Timer – Configurable cooldown period before engine shutdown
  • Instant Auto – Fast transfer to auto mode for critical loads
Event Handling & I/O
  • Event Response Configs (ERCs) – Configurable per-input alarm/shutdown behaviour
  • Digital Inputs (12) – Programmable for commands, status, or system events via Cat ET
  • Analog Inputs (3) – Resistive or voltage mode with built-in and custom sensor maps
  • Digital Outputs (12) – Programmable for status parameters or system events
  • Analog Outputs (2) – Voltage or current mode output
  • PWM Outputs (3) – Duty cycle controlled outputs for fan/valve control
  • SCADA Alarm Bit Packing – Grouped alarm status for efficient remote monitoring
Protection & Safety
  • Overcurrent Protection – Definite time, IEC/IEEE inverse time curves, thermal damage curve
  • Arc Flash Limit – Maintenance mode with reduced trip thresholds
  • Generator Stator Current Limiter – Real-time stator current limiting
  • Breaker Control – Close/trip commands, breaker health monitoring
  • Functional Safety (§42) – IEC 61508 compliant functions: overvoltage, overcurrent, overspeed, E-stop, parity inputs, ECM/governor power removal, fuel gas shut-off, sync breaker control, setpoint calibration
Island Paralleling (§40)
  • Failsafe Mode – Degraded operation when comms lost between units
  • Dead Bus Arbitration – First-on logic to energize a dead bus with configurable priority
  • Synchronization – Voltage, frequency, and phase angle matching with gain tuning procedures
  • Load Sharing – Real (kW) and reactive (kVAR) droop/isochronous sharing with tuning guide
  • LSLD – Automatic load sense/load demand with add/drop logic, N+X redundancy, spinning reserve, proactive add, sequence strategies, event-based redundancy
  • Bus/Intertie Protections – Inter-bus transfer and protection coordination
Utility Paralleling & Advanced Features
  • AMF Control (§21) – Automatic Mains Failure with utility-to-genset and genset-to-utility transfer, utility AC monitoring
  • Base Load / VAR / PF Control (§41) – kW export, reactive load, and power factor control in utility parallel mode
  • IVR (§36) – Integrated Voltage Regulator with PWM excitation, PID tuning, under-frequency roll-off, reactive droop compensation
  • TLR (§37) – Transient Load Relief with operation, setpoint config, and tuning procedures
  • PLC (§38) – Integrated PLC with logic blocks, function blocks, special blocks, debug, PVC file creation, TCP/IP download
  • Web Server (§39) – Built-in HTTP server for remote monitoring
  • Dynamic Gas Blending, Gas Train, Fuel Transfer, Driven Equipment Control

🏭 Balance of Plant (BOP) Software Operations Manual

Covers the auxiliary plant control features that extend the ECS 200 beyond core genset control into Combined Heat and Power (CHP) and containerized power plant applications. The BOP software manages thermal circuits — cooling water, aftercooler, customer heating, exhaust heat recovery — as well as enclosure ventilation and safety systems. Each feature section follows a consistent structure: functional description, control logic flow diagrams, setpoints, diagnostics/status events, I/O mapping, and HMI display requirements.

Cooling Circuit Controls
  • SCAC 3-Way Valve & Pump (§5) – Separate Circuit Aftercooler with PID-controlled 3-way mixing valve, end position sensors, pump follow-up delay timer, immediate stop logic
  • SCAC Radiator (§6) – Aftercooler radiator with VFD (variable frequency drive) or staged single-speed fan switching, temperature setpoint calculation
  • Engine Cooling Valve & Pump (§7) – Jacket water 3-way valve control with pump management
  • Engine Cooling Radiator (§8) – Engine circuit radiator with VFD or staged fan, PID control, temperature setpoint logic
  • Dual Core Radiator (§9) – Combined SCAC + JW radiator control for power-only mode with VFD or stage switching, control deviation calculation
Heat Recovery & CHP
  • Customer Site Heating (§10) – Heating circuit with cooling water heat exchanger, 3-way valve (can be installed in heating circuit or engine cooling circuit), pump control
  • Exhaust Heat Exchanger Bypass (§11) – EHE bypass control for customer heating with exhaust gas valve management, multiple operational scenarios with flow diagrams
  • Exhaust Gas Temp Sensors (§14) – Additional exhaust temperature I/O for CHP monitoring, trip setpoint calculations, and auxiliary temperature sensor configuration
Ventilation & Safety
  • Enclosure Ventilation (§12) – Container/enclosure ventilation with and without circulating air, ventilation fan control, inlet/outlet louver control, standby ventilation mode
  • Smoke/Fire Alarm (§13) – Fire alarm reaction (ventilation shutdown), gas alarm reaction (full ventilation), prioritization logic between fire and gas alarm scenarios
BOP I/O & Integration
  • BOP Ethernet I/O Module (§15) – Remote I/O module configuration for BOP signals
  • Config Code: CHP & Container – I/O mapping for CHP + container applications
  • Config Code: JW & SCAC – I/O mapping for jacket water + aftercooler
  • Config Code: Combined – Full JW, SCAC, CHP, and container I/O mapping
  • HMI display requirements for all BOP DI/DO/AI/AO points

Every BOP feature includes comprehensive control setpoint tables (enable/disable, temperature thresholds, PID gains, timers, hysteresis), diagnostic event definitions (sensor failures, actuator faults, communication errors), status events, data feedback parameters for monitoring, complete I/O allocation tables, and HMI screen layouts showing overview and configuration screens with parameter labels and value formats.

📡 Cat ECS SCADA Modbus Software Manual

Defines the complete Modbus TCP/IP SCADA interface for remote supervisory control and data acquisition from ECS 100 and ECS 200 controllers. This is the integration guide for connecting the Cat ECS to building management systems (BMS), plant SCADA systems, and third-party monitoring platforms. All data is accessed via standard Modbus TCP/IP (port 502) with password-protected read/write access.

Connection & Security Setup
  • IP Configuration – Set via Cat Electronic Technician (ET) service tool
  • Data Link Enabling – Enable SCADA access with password via Cat ET or HMI display
  • HMI Configuration – SCADA settings accessible through HMI configuration screens
  • Password Security – Password-protected write access, password length and complexity rules, lockout behaviour
Data Interpretation & Scaling
  • 1-Word Unsigned Integer – Read/write for simple numerical values with scaling factors
  • 2-Word Unsigned Integer – Read/write for large values (energy meters, hours) spanning two registers
  • Byte Extracting – Unpacking multiple status values from a single register
  • Byte Packing – Combining multiple command values into a single write register
  • State/Status/Command – Enumerated state values, status bit flags, command codes
  • Complex Data – Multi-register structured data for event logs and timestamped records
Genset & Engine Register Maps
  • Genset Ratings – Active group, rated voltage, current, power, frequency
  • Genset AC – Phase voltages, currents, frequency, power factor
  • Genset Power/Energy – Real (kW), reactive (kVAR), apparent (kVA), kWh, kVARh
  • State/Mode/Status – Operating mode, run status, fault codes
  • Start/Stop Commands – Remote start, stop, reset, mode select
  • Load Share / LSLD – Load demand status, kW setpoint, add/drop commands
  • Paralleling Data – Gen #1 through Gen #32 paralleling status registers
  • Engine Data – Service meter, speed, fuel rate, oil pressure/temp, coolant temp, exhaust temps, intake, turbocharger, filter differential, aftertreatment (DEF level, DPF soot, SCR temp), gas data, dynamic gas blending
Events, Network & Special Registers
  • Acknowledge/Reset – Remote event acknowledgement and alarm reset commands
  • Alarm Bit Packing – Hard shutdown, soft shutdown, lamp, horn, display events packed into user-configurable alarm groups
  • Annunciator – Remote annunciator status registers
  • Event Logs – Active event count, system event log (genset + engine), status event log with timestamps
  • Network Status – Communication health for all connected modules
  • Module Information – Firmware versions, serial numbers, hardware IDs
  • Grid Code (§10) – Grid code compliance registers (frequency/voltage ride-through, power ramp rates)
  • Balance of Plant (§11) – BOP-specific registers for CHP thermal management
  • Programmable Cycle Timer & Real-Time Clock – Timer status and RTC read/write

Section 12 provides practical worked examples showing how to read and write Modbus registers including proper scaling, byte ordering, and multi-register reads. The genset paralleling data section supports up to 32 generator units in a single plant, each with its own register block for remote monitoring of the entire paralleling system.

⚡ Best Practices from SCADA Manual §13
  • Batch reads — Read contiguous register blocks in a single FC03 transaction (up to 125 registers). The groups below are arranged to support this.
  • Data prioritization — Poll critical registers every 1 s, slow-changing data every 5–30 s. Use the User Alarm Groups (regs 101–110) for event-driven alerting instead of polling hundreds of event registers.
  • Persistent connections — Keep TCP connections open; do not create a new socket per transaction.
  • Retry with limits — Implement retries for failed reads but limit to 2–3 attempts. Use timeouts of 1000–3000 ms.
  • Two-word registers — Power/energy registers are 32-bit (2 words). MSW is at the lower address. Combine as: value = (MSW << 16) | LSW, then apply scaling and offset.

📐 Scaling Quick Reference
  • Voltage: raw × 1 V/bit = Volts
  • Current: raw × 1 A/bit = Amps
  • Frequency: raw × (1/128) = Hz
  • Power Factor: raw × (1/16384) – 2 = PF
  • Real Power (2-word): ((MSW≪16)|LSW) × 1 W – 2,000,000,000 = Watts
  • Reactive Power (2-word): same as real power but VAR
  • Apparent Power (2-word): ((MSW≪16)|LSW) × 1 VA = VA
  • Energy (2-word): ((MSW≪16)|LSW) × 1 Wh = Wh
  • Oil Pressure: raw × (1/128) = PSI
  • Temperature: raw × (1/32) – 273 = °F
  • Battery Voltage: raw × (1/20) = Volts
  • Engine Speed: raw × 0.125 = RPM
  • Engine Hours (2-word): ((MSW≪16)|LSW) × 0.05 = Hours
  • Percent Load: raw × 0.4 = %
  • Fuel Level: raw × (1/2.56) = %

// Current view (declared above)

// Build PDF URL with page number
function pdfUrl(file, page) {
  return encodeURIComponent(file) + '#page=' + page;
}

// Open PDF at a specific page
function openPdfAt(docKey, page) {
  const doc = docs[docKey];
  window.open(pdfUrl(doc.file, page), '_blank');
}

// Render sections for a document
function renderSections(docKey) {
  const doc = docs[docKey];
  const container = byId(`sections-${docKey}`);
  if (!container) return;

  container.innerHTML = doc.sections.map(section => `
    <div class="section-card" onclick="openPdfAt('${docKey}', ${section.page})">
      <div class="section-header">
        <span class="section-num">${section.num}</span>
        <span class="section-title">${section.title}</span>
      </div>
      <div class="section-desc">${section.desc}</div>
      <div class="section-page-link">
        <span class="section-page">📄 Page ${section.page}</span>
        <span class="open-at-page">Open PDF → p.${section.page}</span>
      </div>
      ${section.subs.length > 0 ? `
        <div class="subsections">
          ${section.subs.map(sub => `
            <div class="subsection" onclick="event.stopPropagation(); openPdfAt('${docKey}', ${sub.page})">
              <span>${sub.title}</span>
              <span class="open-at-page">p.${sub.page} →</span>
            </div>
          `).join('')}
        </div>
      ` : ''}
    </div>
  `).join('');
}

// Show a specific view
function showView(viewKey) {
  currentView = viewKey;

  // Update sidebar
  widget.querySelectorAll('.doc-item').forEach(item => {
    item.classList.toggle('active', item.dataset.doc === viewKey);
  });

  // Update views
  widget.querySelectorAll('.doc-detail').forEach(view => {
    view.classList.remove('active');
  });
  byId(`view-${viewKey}`).classList.add('active');

  // Update topbar
  const titles = {
    overview: 'Overview Dashboard',
    hw: 'Controller Hardware Manual',
    hmi: 'HMI Manual – Paralleling Genset Control',
    sw: 'Software Operations Manual',
    bop: 'Balance of Plant Software Manual',
    scada: 'SCADA Modbus Manual',
    modbus: 'Suggested SCADA Registers — 1-Second Polling List'
  };
  byId('topbarTitle').textContent = titles[viewKey] || '';

  // Update topbar actions
  const actions = byId('topbarActions');
  if (viewKey === 'modbus') {
    actions.innerHTML = `
      <a class="btn btn-secondary" href="${pdfUrl(docs.scada.file, 24)}" target="_blank">📡 Open SCADA Manual → Register Maps</a>
    `;
  } else if (viewKey !== 'overview' && docs[viewKey]) {
    const doc = docs[viewKey];
    actions.innerHTML = `
      <a class="btn btn-primary" href="${pdfUrl(doc.file, 1)}" target="_blank">📄 Open Full PDF</a>
    `;
  } else {
    actions.innerHTML = '';
  }

  // Close mobile sidebar
  byId('sidebar').classList.remove('open');

  // Clear search
  byId('globalSearch').value = '';
  byId('searchResults').classList.remove('visible');
}

// Search functionality - returns granular matches with direct PDF page links
function handleSearch(query) {
  const resultsEl = byId('searchResults');
  const listEl = byId('resultsList');
  const countEl = byId('resultCount');

  if (!query || query.length < 2) {
    resultsEl.classList.remove('visible');
    return;
  }

  const q = query.toLowerCase();
  const allMatches = []; // flat list of every individual hit

  Object.entries(docs).forEach(([key, doc]) => {
    doc.sections.forEach(section => {
      const titleMatch = section.title.toLowerCase().includes(q);
      const descMatch = section.desc.toLowerCase().includes(q);

      // Check section-level match
      if (titleMatch || descMatch) {
        allMatches.push({
          docKey: key,
          docTitle: doc.title,
          docId: doc.id,
          file: doc.file,
          sectionNum: section.num,
          sectionTitle: section.title,
          matchTitle: section.title,
          matchDesc: descMatch ? getSnippet(section.desc, q) : '',
          page: section.page,
          type: 'section'
        });
      }

      // Check every subsection independently
      section.subs.forEach(sub => {
        const subTitleMatch = sub.title.toLowerCase().includes(q);
        // Also match if the parent desc mentions it
        if (subTitleMatch) {
          allMatches.push({
            docKey: key,
            docTitle: doc.title,
            docId: doc.id,
            file: doc.file,
            sectionNum: section.num,
            sectionTitle: section.title,
            matchTitle: sub.title,
            matchDesc: '',
            page: sub.page,
            type: 'subsection'
          });
        }
      });
    });
  });

  const totalMatches = allMatches.length;
  countEl.textContent = `(${totalMatches} location${totalMatches !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found)`;

  if (totalMatches > 0) {
    resultsEl.classList.add('visible');
    listEl.innerHTML = allMatches.map(m => `
      <div class="search-result-item">
        <div class="result-header">
          <div style="flex:1">
            <div class="result-section">
              <span class="result-doc-badge">${m.docId}</span>
              ${m.type === 'subsection' ? `§${m.sectionNum} ${m.sectionTitle} ▸ ` : `§${m.sectionNum} `}${highlightText(m.matchTitle, query)}
            </div>
            <div class="result-doc">${m.docTitle} · Page ${m.page}</div>
            ${m.matchDesc ? `<div class="result-desc">${m.matchDesc}</div>` : ''}
          </div>
          <a class="open-at-page" href="${pdfUrl(m.file, m.page)}" target="_blank" onclick="event.stopPropagation()" style="flex-shrink:0">Open PDF → p.${m.page}</a>
        </div>
      </div>
    `).join('');
  } else {
    resultsEl.classList.add('visible');
    listEl.innerHTML = '<div style="padding:16px;color:#999;text-align:center;">No results found. Try a different term like "ethernet", "load share", or "temperature".</div>';
  }
}

// Get a short text snippet around the matched query in a description
function getSnippet(desc, query) {
  const idx = desc.toLowerCase().indexOf(query);
  if (idx === -1) return '';
  const start = Math.max(0, idx - 60);
  const end = Math.min(desc.length, idx + query.length + 60);
  let snippet = '';
  if (start > 0) snippet += '…';
  snippet += desc.substring(start, end);
  if (end < desc.length) snippet += '…';
  return highlightText(snippet, query);
}

function highlightText(text, query) {
  if (!query) return text;
  const regex = new RegExp(`(${query.replace(/[.*+?^${}()|[\]\\]/g, '\\$&')})`, 'gi');
  return text.replace(regex, '<span class="highlight">$1</span>');
}

// Toggle poll group expand/collapse
function togglePollGroup(id) {
  byId(id).classList.toggle('collapsed');
}

// Mobile sidebar toggle
function toggleSidebar() {
  byId('sidebar').classList.toggle('open');
}
